page: Microsoft Azure HB120-96rs_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the HBrsv2 family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs allocated via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 456.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.75 GB per core. It is built with an Intel processor and does not contain any graphics processing units.

For storage and networking, the instance is bundled with 1452 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations. It is also RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) capable, which facilitates low-latency, high-throughput networking across clustered nodes. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this server type.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high-density memory configuration position this instance for resource-intensive workloads. The inclusion of local storage and RDMA capability makes it suitable for clustered high-performance computing (HPC) applications, large-scale simulations, and parallel processing tasks that require fast node-to-node communication without the overhead of standard network protocols.
description: Microsoft Azure HB120-96rs_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server from the HBrsv2 family, powered by an Intel processor and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 456.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.75 GB of RAM per core. The instance is bundled with 1452 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Designed without GPUs, it features RDMA capability to support low-latency, high-throughput node communication. This hardware profile is optimized for clustered high-performance computing, parallel simulations, and large-scale computational workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources and high-speed interconnects.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 compute instance featuring RDMA networking, substantial local storage, and high-density memory for clustered parallel workloads.
